The 'Output Settings' dialog window is open and visible in the OpenToonz interface.

task_completion.description: Set the output directory to the requested path

task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Settings dialog, the file format dropdown is set to 'mp4', 'mov', 'avi', or 'flv'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation to create the video file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar/window is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created video file in the target directory with a file size of at least 50 KB.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the export
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (menus, dialogs) rather than relying solely on terminal commands to bypass the application.

integrity.description: Agent actually rendered the video from the project file
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of the rendering process (e.g., a render popup or progress bar) rather than the agent just copying or moving a pre-existing video file.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply rename a file to fake the video format
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use terminal commands like 'mv dwanko_run.tnz dwanko_run.mp4' to fake the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The target output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/video_export'. Valid output formats include MP4, MOV, AVI, and FLV. The final exported video file must be at least 50 KB in size.
